meta.title: BMW M40 / M42 / M43 / M44 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: BMW M40 / M42 / M43 / M44 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The M40, M42, M43 and M44 are BMW's original modern four-cylinder petrol engines — simple and robust, but cooling system neglect is their common weakness. Backed by an estimated 150+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: H2: BMW M40/M42/M43/M44 — Our Verdict & Rating
Confidence: 85% confidence — based on enthusiast community consensus and specialist workshop feedback on these older BMW fours [ESTIMATED].
Midpoint of Reconditioned supply range (£1,200–£2,200 → £1,700) — under £3,000 bracket → 20

rows.metric: Most failures (timing chain tensioner, head gasket) are well‑understood and affordable to repair

rows.value: Parts available but some are specialist‑order or NLA; aftermarket support exists

One-line verdict: "The M42/M44 are robust, simple BMW fours — neglect cooling systems and head gaskets fail — buy with documented service history and budget for cooling refresh." (Clause 1: 7 words. Clause 2: 8 words. Clause 3: 8 words. Total: 23 words.)
rows.value: Best For: Classic BMW enthusiasts who want a simple, reliable engine they can work on themselves.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M40/M42/M43/M44 typically takes 7–10 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].

cta.label: → Compare BMW M44 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Cost — "How much does a BMW M44 engine replacement cost?"
items.answer: A reconditioned M42/M44 typically costs £1,200–£2,200 supply only [ESTIMATED], with rebuilt examples reaching £3,500. Fitting adds £500–£900 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the BMW M44 a reliable engine?"
items.answer: The M42/M44 scores 3½★ out of 5 with 85% confidence [ESTIMATED]. They're fundamentally robust and simple, but age‑related cooling system failures and timing chain tensioner wear are well‑known issues to address proactively.

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common BMW M40/M42/M43/M44 failure?"
items.answer: Cooling system failure (water pump, thermostat, radiator) leading to head gasket failure is the most common issue. Timing chain tensioner wear can cause rattles and timing drift on higher‑mileage units. Typically occurs 80,000–120,000 miles [PATTERN DATA].

items.question: Fitment — "Which BMW models use the M42/M44?"
items.answer: The M42/M44 powered the E30 318i (late), E36 316i/318i, E46 318i (M43), E34 518i, Z3 1.9i, and E36 Compact 318ti across 1987–2002. The M40 was the earlier 8‑valve version.

items.question: Longevity — "How long does the BMW M44 last?"
items.answer: With regular cooling system refreshes (water pump every 60k, radiator, thermostat) and timely oil changes, the M42/M44 can exceed 150,000–200,000 miles. Many E36 318is are still on original engines well past 150,000 miles.
